items.title: UniDrive: A Unified Vision-Language and Grounding Framework for Interpretable Risk Understanding in Autonomous Driving
items.topic: UniDrive: A Unified Vision-Language and Grounding Framework for Interpretable Risk Understanding in Autonomous Driving
items.summary: The UniDrive framework, proposed by arXiv researchers, addresses the trade-off between temporal reasoning and spatial precision in autonomous driving scene understanding by combining a temporal reasoning branch with a high-resolution perception branch, published on 2026-06-23. This approach preserves fine-grained spatial details and models scene dynamics from multi-frame visual input. UniDrive's unified visual-language and grounding framework is designed for interpretable risk understanding in autonomous driving. The framework's relevance lies in its potential to improve the safety and efficiency of autonomous vehicles.
items.abstract: The UniDrive framework, proposed by arXiv researchers, addresses the trade-off between temporal reasoning and spatial precision in autonomous driving scene understanding by combining a temporal reasoning branch with a high-resolution perception branch, published on 2026-06-23. This approach preserves fine-grained spatial details and models scene dynamics from multi-frame visual input. UniDrive's unified visual-language and grounding framework is designed for interpretable risk understanding in autonomous driving. The framework's relevance lies in its potential to improve the safety and efficiency of autonomous vehicles.

items.title: Adaptive Cross-Modal Fusion with Sparse Attention for Pedestrian Crossing Intention Prediction
items.topic: Adaptive Cross-Modal Fusion with Sparse Attention for Pedestrian Crossing Intention Prediction
items.summary: Researchers from arXiv introduced ADAPT, a multimodal framework for pedestrian crossing intention prediction, on 2026-07-14. ADAPT jointly models local and global visual context with temporal motion dynamics, processing four spatially aligned visual modalities and ego-vehicle speed, pedestrian bounding boxes, and skeleton pose information. This approach is relevant to autonomous driving as it enhances safety-critical tasks, such as predicting pedestrian behavior. The proposed method achieves accurate pedestrian crossing intention prediction, which is crucial for autonomous vehicles to navigate safely in complex environments.
items.abstract: Researchers from arXiv introduced ADAPT, a multimodal framework for pedestrian crossing intention prediction, on 2026-07-14. ADAPT jointly models local and global visual context with temporal motion dynamics, processing four spatially aligned visual modalities and ego-vehicle speed, pedestrian bounding boxes, and skeleton pose information. This approach is relevant to autonomous driving as it enhances safety-critical tasks, such as predicting pedestrian behavior. The proposed method achieves accurate pedestrian crossing intention prediction, which is crucial for autonomous vehicles to navigate safely in complex environments.

items.title: Handling the Hidden: Occlusion-Aware Motion Prediction for Autonomous Vehicles
items.topic: Handling the Hidden: Occlusion-Aware Motion Prediction for Autonomous Vehicles
items.source: IEEE Open Journal of Intelligent Transportation Systems
items.summary: The IEEE Open Journal of Intelligent Transportation Systems published a paper on Handling the Hidden: Occlusion-Aware Motion Prediction for Autonomous Vehicles on 2026-03-25. This work extends the MultIAMP framework to handle occlusions using real perception inputs, enabling safer navigation in highly interactive and dynamic environments. The proposed approach systematically detects, processes, and anticipates the behavior of occluded areas, which is essential for autonomous vehicles to operate safely in scenarios with limited visibility. The system's evaluation using the CARLA simulator demonstrates its effectiveness in motion prediction and occlusion handling.
items.abstract: The IEEE Open Journal of Intelligent Transportation Systems published a paper on Handling the Hidden: Occlusion-Aware Motion Prediction for Autonomous Vehicles on 2026-03-25. This work extends the MultIAMP framework to handle occlusions using real perception inputs, enabling safer navigation in highly interactive and dynamic environments. The proposed approach systematically detects, processes, and anticipates the behavior of occluded areas, which is essential for autonomous vehicles to operate safely in scenarios with limited visibility. The system's evaluation using the CARLA simulator demonstrates its effectiveness in motion prediction and occlusion handling.
